details.description_raw:
In my role, I played a key part in enabling SAP’s next-generation container services platform within ECS, contributing to the shift from traditional VM-based deployments to cloud-native, Kubernetes-driven architectures. I worked extensively with SAP Gardener to provision and manage Kubernetes clusters across AWS, Azure, and GCP, gaining deep exposure to large-scale, multi-cloud infrastructure. I helped onboard services like Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Connector, and Web Dispatcher onto Kubernetes, using ArgoCD, Argo Workflows, and Helm to implement GitOps-based automated deployments.

I strengthened observability by integrating Prometheus, Grafana, and centralized logging pipelines, and supported container image management through AWS ECR with replication and vulnerability scanning. My work also included contributing to backup and restore setups using Velero, aligning with enterprise-grade security, resilience, and compliance requirements.

This role accelerated my growth as a cloud-native engineer, giving me hands-on expertise in Kubernetes, automation, distributed systems, and modern DevOps practices. It enabled me to combine technical depth with architectural thinking, improving my ability to design scalable services, troubleshoot complex cloud environments, and contribute meaningfully to next-generation platform engineering initiatives.

details.title: Smart Water Monitoring System for Agriculture
details.journal: International Journal of New Technology and Research
details.associated_with: SDM College of Engineering and Technology
details.description_raw: Agriculture is considered as the main source of food and raw materials. In India many farmers are still using the traditional methods for agricultural field. These traditional methods can be replaced using the current technology. This project describes us how irrigation system can be handled by IOT. The growing demand for food in terms of high quality has increased for the industrialization in the agriculture field. mappings.deeper_answer:
This project is centered around the development of an innovative agricultural monitoring system designed to optimize water usage by accurately assessing the environmental needs of various crops. By monitoring temperature, humidity, and soil moisture levels, the system plays a crucial role in water conservation, which is vital for sustainable agriculture.

The system utilizes a distributed network of IoT sensors strategically placed throughout the agricultural fields. These sensors continuously collect data on soil moisture and temperature, providing real-time insights into the growing conditions of the crops. This data is then processed to determine the specific water requirements of each plant, ensuring that they receive the right amount of irrigation.

An essential component of the project is the automated irrigation system, which activates water pumps based on the moisture levels detected by the sensors. By delivering water precisely when and where it is needed, the system minimizes waste and promotes healthier crop growth, ultimately leading to increased yields.

The technology stack for this project includes IoT sensors for data collection, a WiFi modem for connectivity, and a user interface built with PHP and HTML for monitoring and control. The web interface allows farmers to visualize real-time data and manage the irrigation system remotely, enhancing usability and decision-making capabilities.

By combining cutting-edge technology with agricultural practices, this project not only aims to improve efficiency but also addresses the pressing challenge of water scarcity in farming. Ultimately, it empowers farmers to make informed decisions, ensuring sustainable farming practices that conserve resources while maximizing productivity.

mappings.deeper_answer:
At Juniper, we leverage our own Git repositories to streamline the software development lifecycle through a robust automated pipeline. Before any code is committed, a dedicated job is triggered in the pipeline that executes several crucial steps: it performs a checkout of the latest code, applies necessary patches, builds the application, and runs a suite of test scripts. This automated process significantly reduces the manual workload on developers, allowing them to focus on writing quality code rather than spending excessive time on build and test activities.

The integration of Continuous Delivery (CD) practices is fundamental to our approach, aligning with the principles of Software Studio 3.0. CD empowers teams to produce high-quality software in short cycles while ensuring that new features and enhancements can be reliably released at any time. By utilizing techniques such as automated testing and continuous integration (CI), we maintain a high standard in our software development processes.

The automated pipeline not only expedites the build and test phases but also ensures that code quality is upheld. Once the build and tests are successful, our tool automatically triggers the code commit into the Git repositories, seamlessly transitioning the code from development to version control. This methodology minimizes the risks associated with manual deployments, reduces human error, and enables us to push out enhancements and bug fixes to customers rapidly and reliably.

Overall, this project exemplifies our commitment to delivering value through automation, allowing for low-risk software delivery with minimal manual overhead. By fostering a culture of continuous improvement and integration, we ensure that our development teams can respond swiftly to changing requirements while maintaining the highest quality standards in our software products.

projects.title: Pre-Commit Tool [Continuous Integration - CI & Continuous Delivery - CD] || Full Stack Developing & Jenkins
projects.description_raw:
At Juniper, we leverage our own Git repositories to streamline the software development lifecycle through a robust automated pipeline. Before any code is committed, a dedicated job is triggered in the pipeline that executes several crucial steps: it performs a checkout of the latest code, applies necessary patches, builds the application, and runs a suite of test scripts. This automated process significantly reduces the manual workload on developers, allowing them to focus on writing quality code rather than spending excessive time on build and test activities.

The integration of Continuous Delivery (CD) practices is fundamental to our approach, aligning with the principles of Software Studio 3.0. CD empowers teams to produce high-quality software in short cycles while ensuring that new features and enhancements can be reliably released at any time. By utilizing techniques such as automated testing and continuous integration (CI), we maintain a high standard in our software development processes.

The automated pipeline not only expedites the build and test phases but also ensures that code quality is upheld. Once the build and tests are successful, our tool automatically triggers the code commit into the Git repositories, seamlessly transitioning the code from development to version control. This methodology minimizes the risks associated with manual deployments, reduces human error, and enables us to push out enhancements and bug fixes to customers rapidly and reliably.

Overall, this project exemplifies our commitment to delivering value through automation, allowing for low-risk software delivery with minimal manual overhead. By fostering a culture of continuous improvement and integration, we ensure that our development teams can respond swiftly to changing requirements while maintaining the highest quality standards in our software products.

Developers spent excessive time on build and testing workflows
Lack of automated enforcement of code quality before commit
Risk of faulty code entering repositories
solution: Designed and implemented a pre-commit automated CI/CD pipeline
Integrated automated build, patching, and testing before code commit
Ensured only validated code is committed to repositories
Adopted Continuous Delivery (CD) principles for faster release cycles
workflow: If successful → auto commit to repository
impact: Significantly reduced manual effort in build and test processes
Improved code quality through automated validation

projects.title: Smart Water Monitoring System for Agriculture
projects.organization: SDM College of Engineering and Technology
projects.description_raw:
This project is centered around the development of an innovative agricultural monitoring system designed to optimize water usage by accurately assessing the environmental needs of various crops. By monitoring temperature, humidity, and soil moisture levels, the system plays a crucial role in water conservation, which is vital for sustainable agriculture.

The system utilizes a distributed network of IoT sensors strategically placed throughout the agricultural fields. These sensors continuously collect data on soil moisture and temperature, providing real-time insights into the growing conditions of the crops. This data is then processed to determine the specific water requirements of each plant, ensuring that they receive the right amount of irrigation.

An essential component of the project is the automated irrigation system, which activates water pumps based on the moisture levels detected by the sensors. By delivering water precisely when and where it is needed, the system minimizes waste and promotes healthier crop growth, ultimately leading to increased yields.

The technology stack for this project includes IoT sensors for data collection, a WiFi modem for connectivity, and a user interface built with PHP and HTML for monitoring and control. The web interface allows farmers to visualize real-time data and manage the irrigation system remotely, enhancing usability and decision-making capabilities.

By combining cutting-edge technology with agricultural practices, this project not only aims to improve efficiency but also addresses the pressing challenge of water scarcity in farming. Ultimately, it empowers farmers to make informed decisions, ensuring sustainable farming practices that conserve resources while maximizing productivity.
problem_statement: Traditional irrigation systems waste water due to lack of real-time monitoring
Farmers lack insights into soil and environmental conditions
Manual irrigation leads to inefficiency and reduced crop yield
solution: Developed IoT-based smart irrigation system
Automated water delivery based on real-time sensor data
Enabled remote monitoring and control via web interface
workflow: Sensors collect environmental data (soil moisture, temperature, humidity)
Backend processes data and determines irrigation needs
Water pump activated automatically when required
User monitors system via web interface
impact: Improved water conservation
Enhanced crop yield through optimized irrigation
Reduced manual intervention in farming
Promoted sustainable agriculture practices

projects.title: BLE Object Finder Using Android App
projects.organization: SDM College of Engineering and Technology
projects.description_raw:
The Object Finder Android App addresses a common real-life problem faced by individuals in today’s fast-paced world: the frequent misplacement of important items due to busy schedules and distractions. This innovative app leverages Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) connectivity to help users locate their lost belongings, people, or even pets, enhancing peace of mind and efficiency in daily life.

At its core, the Object Finder app operates by pairing with small BLE tags that can be attached to various items, such as keys, wallets, bags, or pet collars. Users can easily attach these tags to any object they wish to keep track of. Once connected, the app enables users to view the last known location of their items on an intuitive map interface, significantly reducing the time and frustration involved in searching for misplaced belongings.

The app features a user-friendly interface that allows for quick setup and easy navigation. Users can customise notifications and alerts, receiving real-time updates when they move out of range of their tagged objects. The app also offers a “Find” button, which activates an audible alarm on the BLE tag, making it easy to locate items even if they are nearby but out of sight.

In addition to locating personal items, the Object Finder app can be utilized to keep track of family members or pets, providing an added layer of security. This functionality is especially valuable in crowded places where keeping track of loved ones can be challenging.

By integrating cutting-edge BLE technology with a practical mobile application, this project not only simplifies everyday life for users but also promotes a sense of security and organization. The Object Finder app is poised to become an essential tool for anyone looking to minimize the stress of losing valuable items in their busy lives.
problem_statement: Frequent misplacement of personal belongings
Difficulty in tracking lost items
Lack of simple real-time tracking solutions
solution: Developed Android app using BLE technology
Enabled real-time tracking of items using BLE tags
Provided alerts and audible detection features
workflow: User attaches BLE tags to items
User receives alerts if out of range
User can trigger alarm to locate item
impact: Improved user convenience in locating items
Reduced time spent searching for lost objects
Enhanced personal security and tracking

Internet of things(IOT) is high performance technology it is offering solutions and innovation to the agriculture field. The main objective of this project is to Help the farmers using IOT technology.

Bridged gap between traditional farming and modern technology
